vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but represented only by the anchor object.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: White rabbit anchor remains, but Pothos foliage is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, damp, and undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White ceramic/mineral shards present in P3; white cylindrical object (sensor/marker) present in P1.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there has been zero vegetative growth or change in the soil surface. The environment is static and devoid of active biological plant life.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. All registered plants are either absent or have perished, leaving only the inert soil and the scale anchor.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white cylindrical sensor/marker in P1 is a new intervention not explicitly detailed in the initial registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline of the pots and compare them against the registry. Upon validation, it is clear that the biome is currently non-functional. There is no evidence of the String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, or Silver Guest. The Pothos is represented only by the white rabbit anchor. The soil appears damp but entirely barren, suggesting a complete loss of the intended botanical specimens.
